summary: Matched a large portion of src/melee/ft/ft_0CDD.c, mainly common fighter item-shooting logic for Ray Gun and Fire Flower. The PR cleaned up decompiled code, promoted several anonymous fn_* symbols to ftCo_* names, typed ftCommonData fields around offsets 0x5A0-0x5B8, corrected function prototypes, and used inline helpers / stack padding / dont_inline pragmas to improve matching. decomp.dev reported 11 newly matched functions and one major improvement: ftCo_800CE650 reached 72.73%, with total matched code +1564 bytes (+0.04%).

key_files.role: Main matching work for ft_0CDD item attack/shoot functions, including Ray Gun and Fire Flower callbacks and animation logic.
Renamed matched local symbols from fn_800CE0CC/fn_800CE14C/fn_800CE1D4/fn_800CE620/fn_800CE650 to ftCo_* names.
Split ftCommonData padding at +5A0 into typed fields x5A0, x5A4, x5A8, x5AC, x5B0, x5B4, and trailing padding x5B8.
Replaced UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations for ft_800D76B8 and ft_800D7770 with void ftCo_GObj* prototypes.

what_changed: Converted many raw fighter_gobj->user_data uses to GET_FIGHTER(fighter_gobj), reducing casts and matching common project style.
Removed stale exploratory comments, unused externs, local assert string/data artifacts, and unused float constants from ft_0CDD.c.

Matched ftCo_800CDE94 by selecting fighter->parts[fighter->ft_data->x8->x10].joint, scaling the input Vec3 by inverse y scale when needed, and calling MTXMultVec(HSD_JObjGetMtxPtr(fighterbone_jobj), &pos, arg2).
Reworked Ray Gun transitions ftCo_800CE0CC and ftCo_800CE14C to call Fighter_ChangeMotionState directly with ftCo motion-state ids and flag 0x0C4C5080, then install ftCo_800CE1D4 as accessory4_cb.

Reimplemented ftCo_800CE1D4 as the Ray Gun accessory callback using throw_flags_b0 as a boolean latch, it_8028E774/it_8028E79C item callbacks, ftCo_800CDE94 coordinate conversion, efSync_Spawn, and ft_80088148 sound/effect calls.

Reworked Fire Flower shoot startup functions ftCo_800CE3E0 and ftCo_800CE480 to use typed p_ftCommonData fields x5A8 and x5AC instead of pointer arithmetic, set itemthrow state fields, and use ftCo_MS_FireFlowerShoot / ftCo_MS_FireFlowerShootAir instead of numeric motion ids 0x98/0x99.
Matched ftCo_800CE620 as a small damage callback that calls it_80292EAC when the fighter has an item.

Substantially decompiled ftCo_800CE650 using inline helpers for repeated Fire Flower effect/projectile timing logic, zeroing throw flag bits, p_ftCommonData timing fields, and PAD_STACK(0x0c); this function improved but remained unmatched at 72.73%.
Simplified Fire Flower animation callbacks by adding zero_throw_flag_b3 and zero_throw_flag_b4 helpers and making ftCo_FireFlowerShootAir_Anim delegate to ftCo_FireFlowerShoot_Anim.
Removed unnecessary function-pointer casts in Fire Flower collision callbacks, passing ft_800CE5A0 and ft_800CE528 directly.
Updated symbols.txt so five previously anonymous fn_* functions are now named ftCo_* at addresses 800CE0CC, 800CE14C, 800CE1D4, 800CE620, and 800CE650.
smart_moves: Typed ftCommonData around offsets used by Fire Flower timing/state logic, replacing suspicious casts like (float)((int)p_ftCommonData + 0x5A8) with p_ftCommonData->x5A8 and related fields.
Used small inline helpers to force repeated flag-clearing and effect-spawning code into the shape the compiler expected, especially around ftCo_800CE650.
Used #pragma dont_inline around functions where inlining behavior affected codegen, and PAD_STACK(0x0c) where stack layout was needed for ftCo_800CE650.
Preserved project naming style by promoting matched common fighter functions to ftCo_* instead of leaving them as fn_* symbols.
Converted magic motion-state values to enum-like names ftCo_MS_FireFlowerShoot and ftCo_MS_FireFlowerShootAir, improving readability without sacrificing matching.
Matched matrix transform behavior by using HSD_JObjGetMtxPtr rather than directly reading fighterbone_jobj->mtx, which also removed local assert-string artifacts.
Factored Fire Flower air animation into the grounded animation implementation once the diff showed identical behavior.
decomp_lessons: When decompiled code is doing pointer arithmetic against a global data struct, type the specific struct fields before forcing expressions; p_ftCommonData offsets x5A4/x5A8/x5AC/x5B0/x5B4 unlocked cleaner Fire Flower code.
Bitfield/latch behavior may match better as bool helpers that clear and return true, as seen with throw_flags_b0, throw_flags_b3, and throw_flags_b4.
For common fighter code, GET_FIGHTER(gobj) and direct typed callbacks can reduce casts and often align with established Melee decomp style.
Do not keep fake assert-string locals or manual mtx field access if the real SDK-style helper call produces the expected codegen; HSD_JObjGetMtxPtr was the better abstraction in ftCo_800CDE94.
Large still-unmatched functions can benefit from extracting forced inline helpers for repeated code regions while leaving the top-level function readable.
Replacing numeric motion-state ids with named ftCo_MS_* constants is safe and useful when the names are already known and codegen remains stable.
A partial match improvement can still be valuable when it confirms struct fields, callback behavior, and symbols for future work.
naming_conventions: Common fighter functions in this address range use the ftCo_* prefix, e.g. ftCo_800CE0CC, ftCo_800CE14C, ftCo_800CE1D4, ftCo_800CE620, ftCo_800CE650.
Action callbacks retain descriptive suffixes such as ftCo_LGunShoot_Anim, ftCo_LGunShootAir_Anim, ftCo_FireFlowerShoot_Anim, ftCo_FireFlowerShootAir_Anim, and *_IASA/*_Coll.
Motion-state constants use ftCo_MS_* names such as ftCo_MS_LGunShootEmpty, ftCo_MS_FireFlowerShoot, and ftCo_MS_FireFlowerShootAir instead of raw numeric ids.
Temporary struct fields in ftCommonData remain offset-based x5A0/x5A4/x5A8/x5AC/x5B0/x5B4 until semantic names are known.
Small local inline helpers used descriptive behavior names such as zero_throw_flag_b0, zero_throw_flag_b3, zero_throw_flag_b4 rather than address names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop was used around functions where inlining had to be suppressed for matching.
PAD_STACK(0x0c) was used in ftCo_800CE650 to control stack layout.

Inline helper extraction was used for repeated ftCo_800CE650 patterns: modulo/timing helper ftCo_800CE650_inline, projectile coordinate helper ftCo_800CE650_inline2, and effect/sound helper ftCo_800CE650_inline3.
Boolean flag-clearing helpers produced the desired branch shape for throw_flags_b0/b3/b4.
Using HSD_JObjGetMtxPtr inside MTXMultVec matched better than direct mtx access and eliminated fake assertion locals.
Adding a manual unused padding local `u8 _[4]` in ftCo_800CE1D4 helped stack/register layout.
Direct callback signatures and removal of unnecessary casts helped match collision wrappers and Fighter_ChangeMotionState calls.
The match report showed 11 newly matched functions and ftCo_800CE650 improved from 0.00% to 72.73%, so the tactics were effective but not complete for the large callback.

review_feedback.feedback: GALE01 matched code increased to 36.25% (+0.04%, +1564 bytes).

Reported 11 new matches in main/melee/ft/ft_0CDD: ftCo_800CE1D4, ftCo_800CE14C, ftCo_800CE0CC, ftCo_800CE480, ftCo_800CDE94, ftCo_800CE3E0, ftCo_FireFlowerShoot_Anim, ftCo_FireFlowerShootAir_Anim, ftCo_800CE620, ftCo_LGunShoot_Anim, and ftCo_LGunShootAir_Anim.
Reported one unmatched-function improvement: ftCo_800CE650 gained 861 bytes and reached 72.73%.
No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Continue matching ftCo_800CE650 after PR 1762; it was still only 72.73% matched.
Investigate whether ftCommonData fields x5A0, x5A4, x5A8, x5AC, x5B0, and x5B4 can receive semantic names from Fire Flower behavior.
Search later PRs for refinements to ft_0CDD.c Fire Flower itemthrow fields and throw_flags bitfield naming.
Check whether ft_800D76B8 and ft_800D7770 prototypes in ft_0D14.h were later renamed or further typed.
